title: Packaging and application device
abstract: The present invention relates to a packaging and application device (1), comprising: - a container (10) comprising a body (11), - a screen (17) that is able to move relative to the body of the container and defines a variable-volume internal space (12) therewith, said internal space (12) containing the product (P) to be picked up, a push exerted on the screen being accompanied by a reduction in the internal volume and the product being output through at least one orifice in the screen, - an applicator comprising a support (23) and an applicator member (21) carried by the support, the support and the screen being designed such that the support can bear against the screen in order to exert said push without the applicator member being substantially compressed against the screen.
claims:
Claims 1. A packaging and application device (1), comprising: - a container (10) comprising a body (11), - a screen (17) that is able to move relative to the body of the container and has a plurality of orifices that define a variable- volume internal space (12) therewith, said internal space (12) containing the product (P) to be picked up, a push exerted on the screen being accompanied by a reduction in the internal volume and the product being output through at least one orifice (33) in the screen, - an applicator (20) comprising a support (23) and an applicator member (21) carried by the support, the support and the screen being designed such that the support can bear against the screen in order to exert said push without the applicator member being substantially compressed against the screen. 2. The device as claimed in claim 1, wherein the screen (17) has a peripheral wall (35), which preferably converges toward the bottom of the container, and the support (23) is designed to bear against this peripheral wall. 3. The device as claimed in either of claims 1 and 2, wherein the support (23) is rigid. 4. The device as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the applicator member (20) is designed to remain at a non-zero distance from the screen (17) when the applicator exerts said push on the screen. 5. The device as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the support (23) is designed to bear against the screen around the entire circumference. 6. The device as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the support is designed to bear against the screen (17) around less than the circumference. 7. The device as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the applicator comprises application elements (21) that are positioned on the applicator such that when the latter exerts said push, the application element(s) is/are situated next to solid regions (32) of the screen. 8. The device as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the applicator comprises a plurality of application elements that are separated from one another 

 at their ends by spaces (26), the orifices (33) opening out next to said spaces (26) when the applicator is bearing on the screen in order to exert said push. 9. The device as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the screen (17) is able to slide axially in the body of the container. 10. The device as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the screen is designed to rest against the bottom of the container at the end of its travel therein. 11. The device as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the applicator comprises bristles (21) that constitute the applicator member. 12. The device as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the applicator comprises spikes that constitute the applicator member. 13. The device as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the screen (17) comprises at its periphery at least one sealing lip (30) that presses against the body (11) of the container. 14. The device as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the product (P) is a cosmetic product, notably a makeup product. 15. A method for loading the applicator of a device (1) as defined in any one of the preceding claims with product, wherein a push is exerted on the screen (17) with the aid of the applicator (20) in order to reduce the volume of the internal space (18) and force the product to exit through the orifices, the push being transmitted to the screen (17) by the support (23) without the applicator member being substantially compressed.
